title: hieracium glaucinum subsp. subpubescens
intro: Hieracium glaucinum subsp. subpubescens, also known as a type of hawkweed, is a plant species that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium glaucinum subsp. subpubescens include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
